arts.ttl: Whoa, Canada! Canucks won’t be bullied by Bayern Munich’s statements
arts.sum: Canada Soccer seems to have had enough of being painted as the bad guy in the injury situation surrounding Bayern Munich star Alphonso Davies. The Athletic reached out to Canada for a retort to the hits that it had been taking all week and Canada Soccer spokesperson Paulo Senra (via @iMiaSanMia ) took exception to the recent comments from Bayern Munich CEO Jan-Christian Dreesen and sporting director Christoph Freund — and called for everyone to put the focus back on Davies’ recovery.

arts.ttl: Bart Verbruggen to Bayern Munich transfer rumor shot down
arts.sum: A rumor emerged on Friday that Bayern Munich, despite recently signing young goalkeeper Jonas Urbig from FC Köln, was still intending to swing for Bart Verbruggen — a major Premier League talent starring for Brighton and the Netherlands national team. Bayern Munich are preparing to make a bid for Brighton goalkeeper Bart Verbruggen this summer after monitoring his stunning progress in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Christoph Freund sets plans for coming months, reveals who the club is talking to for extensions
arts.sum: The plan is for him to come back in the summer and go to the Club World Cup. Hopefully he continues to get a lot of game time in the coming weeks,” Freund said. With so many injuries to deal with on the backline, Freund was asked about the possibility recalling Adam Aznou for this summer’s Club World Cup. The executive acknowledged that would be the case.

arts.ttl: Vincent Kompany ready for shorthanded Bayern Munich to face rugged St. Pauli side
arts.sum: I don’t want to communicate a specific date; hopefully it will be resolved quickly,” said Kompany, who indicated that Jonas Urbig (who was also injured during the break) will start vs. “It starts long before the injuries, with everyone feeling that they’re important and that we need them.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Vincent Kompany won’t commit to timeline for return on Aleksandar Pavlović
arts.sum: Bayern Munich boss Vincent Kompany is under fire in some circles for his handling of injured players, so it might seem natural that the boss was less-than-committal on addressing when midfielder Aleksandar Pavlović will be ready to return from his bout with mono. Hopefully, Bayern Munich — and Pavlović — take the necessary time to allow him to fully recover.

arts.ttl: Report: Kane happy to stay in Munich
arts.sum: With the prospect of becoming the all-time topscorer in the Premier League, Harry Kane will always be linked with a move back to England. For now, however, the English man is set to stay in Munich.

arts.ttl: Leverkusen keep pressure on Bayern with win over Bochum
arts.sum: Victor Boniface scored the decisive goal as Bayer Leverkusen kept up the pressure on Bayern Munich with a 3-1 win over Bochum.
